topic.what: Investigation of how a modified Zuojin Pill formulation modulates glycolytic metabolism in damaged gastric mucosa to facilitate mucosal repair in a gastritis animal model.
topic.how: In vivo animal study combined with metabolomic and proteomic profiling; key glycolytic enzymes (HK2, PKM2, LDHA) and pathway intermediates quantified in gastric tissue; network pharmacology used to identify formula targets overlapping glycolysis regulation; histological repair endpoints assessed.
topic.why_matters: Metabolic reprogramming — specifically aerobic glycolysis — is increasingly recognised as a driver of gastric mucosal inflammation and impaired repair; demonstrating that TCM formula components redirect this metabolic programme opens a new mechanistic dimension beyond direct anti-bacterial or anti-inflammatory effects.

characteristics.what_it_measures: Change in expression/activity of key glycolytic enzymes (HK2, PKM2, LDHA) in gastric tissue
characteristics.value: Significant normalisation of glycolytic enzyme expression toward healthy-tissue levels in treated vs. model animals
characteristics.evidence: Proteomics and Western blot quantification of gastric tissue
characteristics.context: Direction of change (up or down) depends on whether overactivated or underactivated glycolysis characterises the lesion state

characteristics.what_it_measures: Histological restoration of gastric mucosal architecture
characteristics.value: Improved mucosal thickness, gland density, and reduced erosion scores in treated groups
characteristics.context: Repair endpoints correlated with metabolic reprogramming but causal direction not fully established

good_for: Researchers exploring metabolic mechanism-of-action for multi-herb TCM formulas
KB articles connecting TCM pharmacology to Warburg-effect biology in gastric tissue
Hypothesis generation for combining TCM with metabolic inhibitors (e.g., 2-DG)
not_for: Clinical use without human pharmacokinetic and safety data
Studies focused solely on anti-H. pylori activity
requires: Understanding of glycolysis pathway and its role in inflammation
Access to DOI: 10.1016/j.phymed.2024.156255 for full data

novelty: First report linking modified Zuojin Pill action to glycolytic reprogramming rather than purely anti-inflammatory or anti-microbial mechanisms
Integrates network pharmacology target prediction with in vivo metabolic validation
open_problems: Which specific formula constituents drive glycolytic modulation vs. anti-inflammatory effects is not deconvoluted
Whether glycolysis normalisation is sufficient for long-term mucosal integrity or requires additional interventions is unclear
Human metabolomic studies validating the glycolysis signature in gastritis patients are needed
